hypothesis: If the Gumbel-Softmax temperature τ during training is annealed not on a fixed schedule but adaptively in response to the current self-consistency loss—decreasing τ faster when structure recovery is improving and slower when it plateaus—then the encoder will learn sequence distributions that more effectively balance discrete sequence commitment with gradient signal quality, resulting in superior self-consistency TM-scores compared to fixed annealing schedules without sacrificing sequence recovery performance.
